name: Bloop
tagline: AI-powered code search engine written in Rust
description: YC-backed Bloop raised $7.4M to build 'ChatGPT for your code.' The product was killed when the company pivoted to AI agent orchestration in early 2025.
story: Bloop was founded in London in 2021 as a fast, AI-powered code search engine built in Rust. Backed by Y Combinator, the company raised $7.4 million to build what it called 'ChatGPT for your code' — natural language search across entire codebases. The product attracted a developer following and the open-source repository gained traction on GitHub. But as GitHub's native search improved and AI chatbots became capable of understanding code context directly, a dedicated code search tool became redundant. In early 2025, Bloop pivoted to AI agent orchestration tools, effectively killing the code search product that users knew. The GitHub repository was archived on January 2, 2025. Another developer tool that discovered its use case had been absorbed by the platforms.
